title: Here’s why a trade for Brian Thomas Jr. would be a home run move for the Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Brian Thomas Jr. could be a strategic trade target for the Patriots given his potential upside and affordable contract.
Thomas had 1,300 scrimmage yards and 10 TDs in 2024, making him a valuable addition for the Patriots' young roster.
Trading for Thomas is financially advantageous, allowing the Patriots flexibility to pursue additional roster enhancements.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'SNL' on March 7, marking his fourth hosting stint on the show.
Musical guest Gorillaz performed 'Clint Eastwood' to celebrate their 25th anniversary.
Gosling delivered memorable sketches including 'Otezla' and 'Passing Notes' with Ashley Padilla.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, of New Bedford, died after the Yankee Rose capsized off Cape Cod.
The search for the missing second crew member was suspended due to poor weather conditions.
The Yankee Rose was previously involved in a carbon monoxide exposure incident on February 26.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots show interest in Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs as he enters free agency, according to ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.
Doubs, the third-highest-ranked wide receiver, had 55 receptions, 724 yards, and six touchdowns last season with the Packers.
Other teams expressing interest in Doubs include the 49ers, Commanders, and Titans, with projections of a $49 million contract.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Warren Kuchinsky, identified by DNA, was last known alive in the mid-1970s; his skull was found in Bristol, NH in 1986.
The DNA Doe Project used genetic genealogy to solve the 40-year-old case, confirming identity through DNA from a family member.
Investigators found no evidence of foul play in Kuchinsky’s death, according to New Hampshire officials.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of scam involving fake parking violations mimicking official notices with Massachusetts state seal.
Scam notices include a QR code for payments; police advise against scanning or paying without verification.
Victims of the scam should report it immediately; anonymous tips can be sent to the CrimeStoppers tip line.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, from Mansfield was charged with illegal possession of ammunition while on supervised release for a federal conviction.
Authorities found ammunition, firearm parts, and a loaded pistol in Kennedy's home, including materials for M-16 and AR-15 rifles.
Kennedy, a convicted felon with prior convictions for bank robbery and armed robbery, faces up to 15 years in prison if convicted.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward impresses with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games for the Red Sox.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox in RBIs with 7 this spring, showcasing positional versatility in the minors.
Mickey Gasper returns to the Red Sox adding depth at catcher, with 6 hits and a home run this spring.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson remains with the Boston Bruins despite potential trade interest before the deadline.
Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals, scoring 13 goals this season.
A potential three-year contract extension is considered for Arvidsson, despite his pending free agency.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, anticipate reshuffling offensive line for 2026 season.
Jared Wilson expected to move from left guard to center; Patriots target new left guard options.
Possible replacements include free agents Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.
